mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.5 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.5 percent within a 30-day window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and housing starts signals broad-based economic expansion that lifts corporate profit margins. The joint positive movement in GDPC1 and HOUST predicts positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (GDPC1 change × HOUST change) shows a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with 20-day SPX returns across three consecutive rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery phases, rising industrial production indicates capacity utilization improvements that directly translate into higher earnings per share. This supply-side signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day SPX returns show no positive correlation with INDPRO growth rates when the economy is classified as post-recession, or the predictive R-squared falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales and financial assets indicate increasing household liquidity and consumption demand, which mechanically lifts corporate top-line growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Monthly RSAFS growth shows a negative or zero correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns across a 10-year backtest, with a t-statistic below -1.5.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises above 2.5 percent year-over-year, inflation expectations trigger a repricing of discount rates, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in more than 50% of months where CPIAUCSL annualized growth crosses above 2.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: The divergence between nonfarm payrolls growth and the unemployment rate captures labor market structural shifts. When PAYEMS expands rapidly while UNRATE remains sticky above 6.0 percent, productivity concerns weigh on equity valuations, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and elevated UNRATE fails to predict negative 60-day SPX returns in a walk-forward test, or the out-of-sample Sharpe ratio drops below zero.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion environments, housing permit data leads equity markets by capturing consumer confidence shifts. Rising HOUST values directly precede positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day window when the business cycle peaks.
failure_condition: HOUST month-over-month changes show no predictive power for 5-day SPX returns during NBER-defined late-cycle quarters, with a directional accuracy below 55%.
